# Ko and superko examples on 5x5 (auto-generated by src/ko_probe.zig)

X = black, O = white. Coordinates: columns A..E left→right, rows 1..5 top→bottom.
Superko = a whole-board repetition whose cycle is longer than simple ko's
2-ply recapture; only the full-history rule forbids it.

## Sample of legal play from the empty board

- nodes walked: 40000709 (cap 40000000), line-depth cap 80
- simple-ko bans (2-ply): 13908
- **superko bans (cycle >= 3): 414**
- longest cycle seen: 5 plies

---

# Replayable examples

Captured 6 superko and 6 simple-ko cycle(s), each verified by replay.

### SUPERKO example 1 — cycle length 3 — replay check: PASS ✓

Start from (X=black, O=white), then play the moves:

```
 . . . . .
 . . . . .
 . . . . .
 . . . . .
 . . . . .
```

1. X A1   2. O B1   3. X C1   4. O D1   5. X E1   
6. O A2   7. X B2   8. O A1   9. X C2   10. O D2   
11. X E2   12. O A3   13. X B3   14. O C3   15. X D3

The next move — O A1 — recreates the whole-board position from 3 plies back, so it is **illegal under positional superko** (a simple-ko rule would NOT catch this).
